disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapid, shallow breathing developed over 12 hours following a 2-day mild upper-respiratory cold
trigger_question_examples: When did the fast breathing start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He had a runny-nose cold for a couple of days and then over the last 12 hours his breathing got really fast and shallow and he's gone downhill.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: On each breath in, the abdomen pushes out while the chest wall draws inward (see-saw breathing)
trigger_question_examples: What does his chest do when he breathes in?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: When he breathes in, his tummy pushes out but his chest actually caves inward — it looks like it's working against itself.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Wet gurgling upper-airway sound with a weak, ineffective cough — unable to clear secretions
trigger_question_examples: Can he cough the mucus up?
Is there a rattle in his throat?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a wet rattly gurgle in his throat and his cough is so weak it does nothing — he can't get the mucus out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete feeding refusal today with weak suck and swallow
trigger_question_examples: How is his suck and swallow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He won't take any feed at all today and his suck and swallow seem really weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Infant appears exhausted and floppy but remains alert and visually tracking
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He looks worn out and completely floppy, but his eyes are still following us around the room.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Known severe congenital floppy-muscle condition from birth; relies on diaphragm to breathe
trigger_question_examples: Does he have any medical conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: He was diagnosed as a baby with a severe floppy-muscle condition — spinal muscular atrophy type 1 — he's always been very floppy and his chest muscles are weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Family has a home mechanical cough-assist device and a written care plan, but are hesitant to use the device forcefully
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any equipment at home for his chest?
Do you have a care plan for emergencies?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: We have his cough-assist machine here and his SMA care plan, but we're frightened of using the machine too hard on him.
